mission.title: vllm: [RFC] Automatic Prefix Caching
mission.description: [RFC] Automatic Prefix Caching. This issue affects 58+ users.

steps.title: Identify vllm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your vllm installation:
```bash
vllm version
vllm status 2>&1 | head -20
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review vllm configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant vllm configuration:
This RFC discusses our plan for implementing automatic prefix caching in vLLM.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [RFC] Automatic Prefix Caching
steps.description:
The goal of this diff is to allow for automatic prefix caching. This is done by adding an additional level of indirection between the logical and physical blocks which allows for identical logical blocks to map to the same physical block. 

This diff replaces the existing manual prefix caching
```yaml
hash(prefix tokens, tokens in this block)
```

steps.title: Confirm [RFC] Automatic Prefix Caching is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test vllm to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The goal of this diff is to allow for automatic prefix caching. This is done by adding an additional level of indirection between the logical and physical blocks which allows for identical logical blocks to map to the same physical block. 

This diff replaces the existing manual prefix caching mechanism added in #1669

Before:
```
Logical block table --> physical block table.
codeSnippets: hash(prefix tokens, tokens in this block)
Logical block table --> hash table --> physical block table.
Logical block table --> physical block table.

prerequisites.description: A working vllm installation or development environment.
